commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a3992f4e620cb00bb0fe705689dbfeb71033f96a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Gateway `chat.send` abort registration before attachment preprocessing, adds terminal `timeout`/`error` ACK handling and dedupe behavior, updates Control UI terminal-ACK adoption logic, widens a forwarded skills revision result schema, documents the ACK change, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +308, Tests +1237, Docs 0. Total +1545 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main registers the chat abort controller only after awaited attachment preprocessing, leaving the reported immediate-abort race; the PR adds deterministic regression coverage, but I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs human handling because the external PR still needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer compatibility review in addition to the small code fix.

target.title: Fix chat.abort during attachment send preprocessing
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the `chat.abort` / `chat.send` race from #84176 by registering the chat run abort controller **before** awaited attachment/model preprocessing, so an immediate abort can find and cancel the just-started run.
- An aborted-before/at-dispatch send now acks the **terminal** `{runId,status:"timeout"}` (was a non-terminal `{status:"started"}`). The non-terminal ack let the Control UI resurrect the cancelled run and left `agent.wait` hanging until its own timeout; the terminal ack records the abort outcome immediately. Accepted live sends still ack `{status:"started"}` and duplicates still ack `{status:"in_flight"}`.
- Post-dispatch abort detection reads **this run's own AbortController signal**, not the shared `chatAbortedRuns` marker. The marker is keyed by `clientRunId` (= idempotency key) and is reused by any prior/concurrent same-key run, so a stale or cross-run marker would otherwise misclassify a fresh same-key send as aborted (false `timeout` / a real error swallowed). `abortChatRunById` is the sole production setter of both the marker and the controller abort, so genuine current-run aborts are detected identically while stale markers no longer leak across runs. The shared marker is left untouched, preserving existing abort-tail event suppression.
- Conflict repair also preserves upstream chat send timing metadata and protects `sessions.abort` terminal dedupe from being overwritten by a late pre-dispatch send ack.
- Scope boundary: this **does** widen the `chat.send` ack `status` union to add the terminal `"timeout"`/`"error"` values (Control UI `ChatSendAckStatus` and the forwarded `SkillsProposalRequestRevisionResultSchema`, which carries the `chat.send` ack via `skills.proposals.requestRevision`). No new RPC method, no config/auth/security/network change, no channel adapter change.

AI-assisted; contributor manually reviewed and is responsible for the change.

## Linked context

- Closes #84176
- Related #84274
- Current candidate head / proof head for this update: `a3992f4e620cb00bb0fe705689dbfeb71033f96a`
- Current base checked locally: `1a3ce7c2a8da266cd0bb2844d44ba5af3942908b`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed:

`chat.abort` called immediately after an attachment `chat.send` now finds and cancels the just-started run during attachment preprocessing, and the send acks the terminal `{runId,status:"timeout"}` so no stale active run is left for the Control UI to re-adopt and no `agent.wait` waiter hangs. A fresh send that reuses an idempotency key whose prior run was aborted (after the short `chat:<runId>` dedupe has expired but while the longer-lived abort marker lingers) is reported by its own outcome (`ok`/`error`), not a stale-marker `timeout`.

- Real environment tested:

Two layers. (1) The registration-order fix (abort reaches the in-preprocess run, no stale active run remains afterward) was validated on a local OpenClaw dev Gateway over loopback WebSocket (protocol 4, isolated temporary OpenClaw home under `/tmp`, device-signed operator GatewayClient with `operator.read`/`operator.write`). (2) The terminal-ack ack-shape change, the per-run-signal abort detection, the UI adopt/normalizer gate, and the widened forwarded schema are covered by focused, deterministic regression tests that drive the real `chat.send` handler and the real UI controller/app-chat send path, plus core/test typechecks, formatter, and independent read-only contract reviews of the branch.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# Gateway + protocol-schema suites (real chat.send handler, abort interleavings, schema accept/reject)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts \
  packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.test.ts

# Control UI suites MUST run via the ui workspace vitest
( cd ui && node ../node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
    src/ui/app-chat.test.ts src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts )

# Types + format
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 $(git diff --name-only 1a3ce7c2a8da266cd0bb2844d44ba5af3942908b...HEAD)
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
candidate head: a3992f4e620cb00bb0fe705689dbfeb71033f96a
gateway suite (directive-tags + gateway-server-chat-b + 2 siblings): 292 tests passed
protocol schema suite (agents-models-skills): 6 tests passed
Control UI suite (app-chat + controllers/chat): 206 tests passed
tsgo core / test:src / test:ui: passed (exit 0, empty output)
oxfmt --check: all matched files use the correct format
commit identity/signature: all 11 PR-range commits authored/committed as nxmxbbd noreply and SSH-signed
independent read-only contract reviews of the per-run-signal change: no blocking findings
```

Asserted behavior in the regression suite (the real handler/controller, not mocks of the logic under test):
- abort-before/at-dispatch `chat.send` acks `{runId,status:"timeout"}` and writes a terminal `{status:"timeout",summary:"aborted",stopReason}` dedupe; a duplicate same-key send then observes the terminal `timeout`.
- a fresh same-key send with a stale abort marker present (its own run never aborted) completes `ok` / surfaces a real error, and the shared marker is left intact.
- the Control UI does not adopt (resurrect) a run on a terminal `timeout`/`error` ack; `normalizeChatSendAck` preserves terminal statuses; the deferred session-refresh is not seeded for terminal acks.
- `SkillsProposalRequestRevisionResultSchema` accepts `started|in_flight|ok|timeout|error` and rejects unknown statuses.

- Observed result after fix:

The immediate abort cancels the in-preprocess run and the send acks terminal `timeout`; the Control UI treats that ack as a finished run instead of re-adopting it, and `agent.wait` resolves instead of hanging. After a send settles, a second abort returns `aborted:false`/`runIds:[]` and history stays empty (no stale active run). A same-idempotency retry after `sessions.abort` returns the terminal `{status:"timeout",stopReason:"rpc"}` dedupe before and after the original suspended send settles. A fresh same-key send long after an earlier abort is reported by its own outcome.

- What was not tested:

No fresh end-to-end live-Gateway capture was taken for the terminal-ack ack-shape change or the per-run-signal detection; those are covered by the deterministic regression suite and the independent reviews. Not tested: manual browser UI reproduction, remote/non-loopback Gateway, and real model-provider dispatch after preprocessing (the abort proofs intentionally cancel before dispatch). Post-push CI is not yet claimed green for the refreshed head.

## Known trade-offs / out of scope

- The abort marker conflates "this run id was aborted" suppression with same-id liveness. This change fixes the post-dispatch dedupe decision via the per-run signal without touching that marker; a fuller fix (a per-registration generation/epoch token on the marker) is a separate, larger refactor and is out of scope here.
- Same-key idempotency reuse remains inherently ambiguous: in a rare concurrent same-key retry, a first run's sticky cancellation dedupe can keep `chat:<key>` at `timeout` even after a later same-key send writes its own outcome. This is unchanged dedupe behavior, not introduced here.
- The terminal-ack approach is local to `chat.send` (which is always a `chat-send` run) and depends on dispatch reaching its `.then`/`.catch`; hoisting the terminal write into `abortChatRunById` would also close a theoretical never-settles hang, but that is a broader change deferred out of this PR.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, intended: immediate abort now reaches the in-preprocess send, and an aborted send acks terminal `{status:"timeout"}` instead of non-terminal `{status:"started"}`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

What is the highest-risk area?

Cancellation/idempotency timing around early abort registration, post-dispatch abort detection, and dedupe caching.

How is that risk mitigated?

Abort detection uses the per-run controller signal (only this run's genuine abort sets it; verified `abortChatRunById` is the sole controller-aborter and marker-setter), the shared marker is left untouched so abort-tail suppression is preserved, response shapes are preserved for non-aborted sends, `chat:<runId>` and `agent:<runId>` dedupe namespaces stay separate, and focused regression coverage exercises the abort interleavings, the stale-marker case, the UI adopt gate, and the forwarded schema.

## Tests and validation

Commands run on candidate head `a3992f4e620cb00bb0fe705689dbfeb71033f96a` (see "Exact steps" above for the full invocations):

- Gateway suite (directive-tags + gateway-server-chat-b + 2 siblings) --- 292 tests passed.
- Protocol schema suite (agents-models-skills) --- 6 tests passed.
- Control UI suite (app-chat + controllers/chat), run via the `ui` workspace vitest --- 206 tests passed.
- `tsgo` core + test:src + test:ui --- passed (exit 0, empty output).
- `oxfmt --check` over changed files --- all matched files use the correct format.
- Commit identity/signature over the PR range --- all 11 commits authored/committed as `nxmxbbd` noreply and SSH-signed.
- Independent read-only contract reviews of the per-run-signal change --- no blocking findings.

Regression coverage added/updated in this update:

- abort-before/at-dispatch `chat.send` acks terminal `timeout` and writes the terminal abort dedupe; duplicate same-key send observes the terminal `timeout`.
- a fresh same-key send with a stale abort marker present completes `ok` / surfaces a real error, with the marker left intact.
- Control UI does not adopt a run on a terminal `timeout`/`error` ack; `normalizeChatSendAck` preserves terminal statuses; deferred session-refresh is skipped for terminal acks and still seeded for live (`started`/`in_flight`) acks.
- `SkillsProposalRequestRevisionResultSchema` accepts the widened ack statuses and rejects unknown ones.
- (Pre-existing, retained) abort by run id during preprocessing, duplicate-send `in_flight`, preprocessing-failure caching, pre-dispatch media cleanup, and `sessions.abort` sticky terminal dedupe.

## Current review state

Next action: push the refreshed candidate head `a3992f4e620cb00bb0fe705689dbfeb71033f96a` to the existing PR branch and let normal post-push CI/bot feedback run. Local guardrails pass and independent read-only contract reviews of the per-run-signal change found no blocking findings.

Waiting on: post-push CI/bot feedback on the refreshed head.
target.author: nxmxbbd

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
